messages.content:
You are a technical writing assistant. You rephrase Python function docstrings while preserving their exact semantics.
Rules:
- Keep the function signature (name, parameters, return type annotation) exactly unchanged.
- Keep any import statements exactly unchanged.
- Keep doctest examples (lines starting with >>> or ...) exactly unchanged.
- Rephrase only the natural language description inside the docstring.
- Make each variant meaningfully different from the others.
- Return a JSON array of 4 strings, each containing one complete paraphrased function. No markdown fences, no other text.

def exchange(lst1, lst2):
    """In this problem, you will implement a function that takes two lists of numbers,
    and determines whether it is possible to perform an exchange of elements
    between them to make lst1 a list of only even numbers.
    There is no limit on the number of exchanged elements between lst1 and lst2.
    If it is possible to exchange elements between the lst1 and lst2 to make
    all the elements of lst1 to be even, return "YES".
    Otherwise, return "NO".
    For example:
    exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 2, 3, 4]) => "YES"
    exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 5, 3, 4]) => "NO"
    It is assumed that the input lists will be non-empty.
    """

    """This function takes two lists of numbers and determines if it is possible to exchange elements between them so that lst1 consists solely of even numbers. For example:
exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 2, 3, 4]) => "YES"
exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 5, 3, 4]) => "NO"
The input lists are assumed to be non-empty."""", "def exchange(lst1, lst2):

exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 2, 3, 4]) => "YES"
exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 5, 3, 4]) => "NO"
It is guaranteed that the input lists are not empty."""", "def exchange(lst1, lst2):
    """This implementation determines if, by exchanging elements between two input lists of numbers, we can make lst1 contain only even values. Exchanges can involve any number of elements. The function returns "YES" if this goal is attainable, and "NO" otherwise. For example:
exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 2, 3, 4]) => "YES"
exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 5, 3, 4]) => "NO"
The lists provided as input are assumed to have at least one element."""", "def exchange(lst1, lst2):
    """The function evaluates whether element swaps between two number lists can result in lst1 being all even. There is no cap on the quantity of elements swapped. Return "YES" if achievable, "NO" if not. For example:
exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 2, 3, 4]) => "YES"
exchange([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 5, 3, 4]) => "NO"
Both input lists are guaranteed to be non-empty.""""]
